//: AEMaaCS OSGi config for the Sling 'default' thread pool.
This is the pool that fires Quartz-scheduled jobs including
com/adobe/granite/workflow/timeout/job — i.e. workflow auto-advancement.
Deploy under ui.config/src/main/content/jcr_root/apps/<project>/osgiconfig/config.author/
AEMaaCS CAVEAT: some Sling core configs are reserved / filtered by the
platform. After deploying, verify the pool actually picked up your values:
/system/console/status-threads  → look for the 'default' pool row, confirm
maxPoolSize and block policy match.
If the numbers don't change, this PID is Adobe-managed on your environment —
do NOT try to work around it. Open an Adobe Support ticket describing the
saturation evidence (thread dump + thread-pool status) and ask Engineering
to lift the pool size or change block policy for that environment.
name: default
//: Minimum queued tasks beyond which new threads are created.
Maximum concurrent threads. 20 is the AEM default; raise if you have
many custom schedulers. Workflow auto-advance needs headroom here.
Core (always-alive) pool size.
Idle thread keepalive in seconds.
RUN = caller-runs (safe; degrades gracefully under pressure).
ABORT = rejects the task — silently drops workflow timeout jobs when pool is full.
Leave RUN unless you have a specific reason to drop tasks.
Shutdown waits for queued tasks if true.
Thread priority: NORM is correct for workflow / scheduler workloads.
Daemon threads die with the JVM; appropriate for a pool-managed worker.
